nl_assertions: States the diagnosis explicitly using the term "insomnia" and distinguishes it from normal short-term sleep disturbance.

Elicits core insomnia features: difficulty initiating sleep, difficulty maintaining sleep, early morning awakening, and/or non-restorative sleep, plus daytime impairment (fatigue, sleepiness, mood, cognition, functioning).

Assesses duration and frequency sufficient to support an insomnia disorder framework (e.g., symptoms occurring at least several nights per week and persisting for weeks) while acknowledging uncertainty if duration criteria are not met.

Screens for common contributors and secondary causes: stress/anxiety/depression symptoms, pain, nocturia, GERD, restless legs symptoms, witnessed apneas/snoring, shift work/circadian disruption, and medication/substance contributors (caffeine, alcohol, nicotine, stimulants, steroids, decongestants).

Performs safety checks before any prescribing is suggested: asks about medication allergies (critical), checks for key contraindications to sedative-hypnotics if discussed (critical; e.g., pregnancy, severe OSA/respiratory disease, history of substance use disorder, severe hepatic impairment, older age/fall risk), and considers drug interactions (non-critical).
Explains the diagnosis in patient-friendly language and includes the word "insomnia" during the explanation.
Explains first-line treatment emphasizing non-pharmacologic therapy and must include both "CBT-I" and "sleep hygiene" in the treatment discussion.

Provides at least brief sleep hygiene counseling (regular schedule, limit caffeine/alcohol, reduce evening screens, optimize sleep environment, avoid long naps) and sets expectations that CBT-I is the most effective long-term approach.

Avoids unsafe recommendations (e.g., combining sedatives with alcohol, driving when drowsy) and advises urgent evaluation if red flags emerge (suicidal ideation, severe depression/mania, concerning OSA symptoms with high-risk features).
Uses shared decision-making, checks understanding, and offers follow-up timing or next steps even if not using a follow-up tool.
